content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    GEORGE DEUKME]IAN, Governor

PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION
505 VAN NESS AVENUE
SAN FRANCISCO, CA  94102-3298


September 26, 1990


Ms. Louisa Menchaca
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA. 95814

Re: Invitation to Participate on Panel at Comet '91

Dear Ms. Menchaca:

     In accordance with our telephone conversation on Friday,
September 14, 1990, I am writing to request a written opinion on
the propriety of my participating on behalf of the California
Public Utilities Commission on a panel at the Comet '91
Conference scheduled for January 29, 1991, in Washington, D.C.

     The People and the Public Utilities Commission of the State of
California ("CPUC") recently won a major court victory in
California v. FCC, 905 F.2d 1217 (9th Cir. 1990). The
significance of this court decision and what it bodes for the
future of the telecommunications industry are matters of
considerable interest to corporations, associations, legislators
and state and federal regulators across the nation.

     In part because of the CPUC's leading role in the court case, a
company called World Expo Corporation, 111 Spear Street,
Framingham, MA 01701, a subsidiary of International Data Group
(IDG), has invited the CPUC to provide a representative to
participate in a panel at the Comet '91 Conference concerning
the legal implications and developments likely to occur in the
industry following the California v. FCC court decision. CPUC
Legal Division management has determined that it would be an
appropriate person to participate in the panel. It is also
possible that another CPUC lawyer might later be substituted to
participate in my place.

     Comet '91 is a major conference, and participation by a
representative of the CPUC will enhance California's reputation
and role as a leading state regulatory agency in the
telecommunications industry nationwide. The moderator of the
panel is slated to be a U.S. Senate Commerce Committee Staff
Counsel, and participants include the Chief of the FCC's Common
Carrier Bureau and counsel for leading telecommunications
carriers. Other conference participants (some are invited, most
have already accepted) include U.S. Congressmen, FCC
Commissioners, counsel for AT&T, representatives of the U.S.
Department of Justice, the legal assistant to FCC Chairman Sikes,
the Chairman of the D.C. Public Service Commission,
representatives of the NTIA, several U.S. Senate Commerce

Ms. Louisa Menchaca
September 26, 1990

Page 2


Committee Staff Counsel, a representative of the National League
of Cities, the General Counsel of the FCC and the Chief of the
FCC's Office of Engineering and Technology. A copy of the
tentative conference schedule and list of speakers is attached.

In order facilitate participation by a CPUC representative, the
organizers of Comnet '91 have offered to reimburse the CPUC for
the cost of the participant's round-trip coach non-stop air fare
from California to Washington, D.C., and for one night's hotel
rate (government or conference rate, if possible), plus
applicable taxes. It seems obvious that the value to World Expo
Corporation of having a CPUC representative speak and participate
in the panel is worth more than this cost; otherwise World Expo
Corporation would not have offered to assume the cost.

Under this scenario, the CPUC participant's round trip to
Washington, D.C., on January 28 and 29, 1991, would be treated as
normal out-of-state travel on state business, with the
participant's expenses reimbursible by the state pursuant to a
travel expense claim consistent with applicable state
regulations. The CPUC would then bill World Expo Corporation to
recover the air fare and hotel expense.

I am unable to perceive any conflict-of-interest or other problem
with this arrangement, either for me or for the CPUC, but would
appreciate your opinion. If you need further information, please
telephone me at 415-557-2563 (ATSS No. 597-2563). If you wish to
contact World Expo Corporation, you may telephone Glenn Gaudet at
508-879-6700, ext. 246.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely yours,

Mark Fogelman
Public Utilities Counsel IV

FOG:cip

cc: Janice E. Kerr, General Counsel, CPUC
Edward W. O'Neill, Asst. General Counsel, CPUC
Glenn Gaudet, World Expo Corporation
